subcodes.title: Words to a journal editor who wrote against the Faith, spoken on 1913-05-21
subcodes.first_line: If I knew that thou wouldst not think that I am afraid, I would have sent thee a gift for writing such a book against the Bahá'í movement. Thy example is like unto the ant who scaled a high, sturdy poplar tree
subcodes.subjects: @humor; jokes, @growth of the Cause, @stories; anecdotes, @rejection, opposition and persecution
